codeReview.summary: Re-review of the prior failure shows both blocking findings are now resolved. For VAL-CROSS-004, the latest worker transcript/handoff includes real TTY cancellation evidence (tuistory Ctrl+C at prompt stages), explicit residue checks, and successful follow-up non-interactive reruns. For VAL-CROSS-005, the latest run includes interactive-flow evidence plus strengthened semantic parity assertions in `cross-flow-parity.test.ts` across equivalent intent/flag forms. No blocking defects found in this fix commit.

issues.description: The SIGINT test uses a fixed-delay `setTimeout(..., 50)` kill but does not assert that the process was actually interrupted by signal; if the process exits before the timer, the test can still pass without proving SIGINT-path execution.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Interactive validation guidance exists, but shared library docs still lack a concrete tuistory recipe/pattern for reproducible TTY evidence collection.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl:34 shows ad-hoc discovery/use (`tuistory --help` and many `tuistory -s ...` commands). `.factory/library/user-testing.md:22-25` requires interactive checks but `.factory/library/` has no `tuistory` references (`rg "tuistory" /var/home/milton/agentfiles/.factory/library` => no matches).

summary: Re-reviewed feature `rulesync-architecture-cross-flow-parity` against prior fail evidence and latest commit `b19288df08ab78a07fd0f19b434ac5a77dedeb86`. Prior blockers are resolved: VAL-CROSS-004 now has real interactive cancellation + rerun/no-residue evidence, and VAL-CROSS-005 now has interactive evidence plus stronger semantic parity checks. Result: PASS with one non-blocking test-rigor note.
